Medilodge of Plymouth

CCN: 235507 · Plymouth, MI 48170 · Wayne County

Overview

Address
395 W Ann Arbor Trail, Plymouth, MI 48170
Phone
7344533983
Certified beds
39
Avg daily residents
37 (94% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1991-06-05
Setting
Urban
5 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 5/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 5/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.05 3.69 4.24 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 1.21 0.65 1.27 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.68 0.82 0.71 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.16 2.23 2.26 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.89 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.11 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.28
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.67
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.44
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
63.6%
annual
RN turnover
22.2%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.31
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-07-17 1 1 0 0 0 0
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-07-25 7 6 1 24 1 24
Total weighted health score
6.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (17)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

4 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
13 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2025-07-17 —
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-18
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-18
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. B 2024-07-25 2024-08-18
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-18
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-18
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-18
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2024-07-25 —
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2023-04-20 2023-05-12
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2023-04-20 2023-05-12
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. F 2023-04-20 2023-05-12
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. F 2023-04-20 2023-05-12
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-04-20 2023-05-12
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. F 2023-04-20 2023-05-12
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. F 2023-04-20 2023-05-12
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2023-04-20 2023-05-12
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2023-04-20 2023-05-12

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
